marketState: neutral — extreme near-term heat demand battles storage surplus and poor seasonal odds
summary: Near-term weather demand is exceptional: both the 1-7D and 8-16D forecast windows are above the 5-year maximum, with current gas-weighted demand at 184.8 BCF/d — +47% above the 5-year normal and still surging (+27.2 in 24 hours). This is the dominant near-term bullish catalyst, amplified by managed money sitting at only the 18th 5-year percentile net short (−82.7K contracts), creating classic short-squeeze conditions. Offsetting this: price is already slightly above fair value ($3.306 vs model fair of $2.960, though within the $2.46–$3.46 band), storage sits +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a slightly bearish injection surprise, and seasonal odds are poor — only 19% probability of higher prices in 4 weeks with a typical 4-week move of −1.8%. Weekly technicals confirm the structural downtrend (50 EMA below 200 EMA, RSI 46.8). The tactical framework is short-term long on weather and short-squeeze risk, then fade the rally for a multi-week short as heat normalizes and the storage surplus reasserts. The futures curve is available this snapshot and confirms near-term mild backwardation (Aug–Oct) then a steep winter contango (Jan 2027 at $4.452, +$1.15 vs front), consistent with normal seasonal structure with no near-term bullish curve signal beyond the spot heat premium.

Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average continues to grow as the injection season peaks, and fair value rises only to $3.163 in 21 days vs current price of $3.306, implying price must converge downward over the medium term. Weekly EMA structure (50 below 200) confirms the broader downtrend. Entry is staged at higher levels to allow the near-term weather squeeze to exhaust before pressing the short.
longTerm.invalidation: Price breaks and holds above $3.65 (above current fair-value band top with buffer); managed money aggressively covers shorts driving net positioning above the 40th 5-year percentile; or storage draws accelerate materially below seasonal norms reducing the 5-year surplus.

Intraday and 4-hour charts support the direction (both trending bullish with 50 EMA above 200 EMA, RSI 49.9–54.4), and price remains within the fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46) with room to press toward the band top before becoming overvalued.
